log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable with a GUI, packed with UPX and exhibiting overlay behavior. It has been analyzed in two sandboxes: CAPA and VirusTotal Jujubox.
The sample does not appear to have any network activity, registry persistence keys, or process creation. However, it does drop 18 files.
The sample drops 18 files, all of which appear to be legitimate software installers or cracked versions of software. This suggests that the sample may be a dropper or a spreader.
The sample does not appear to create any new processes, load any notable modules, or create any mutexes. It also does not make any highlighted API calls.
The sample does not appear to create any registry persistence keys, suggesting that it may not be designed to persist on the system after execution.
The sample does not appear to have any network activity, including IP traffic, DNS lookups, or HTTP conversations.
The sample exhibits behaviors consistent with being packed with a generic packer, containing a loop, and having a resource section. These behaviors are indicative of anti-analysis techniques.
